Render the local player's body directly in first person and include the full player in water, reflections, shadows, and indirect lighting.
caustica.options.rt.firstPerson.disableV: Hide Minecraft's screen-space first-person hands and held item while the ray-traced first-person body is active.
Fine-tune the tested first-person pose along the player's facing direction. Zero is the recommended placement.
caustica.options.rt.firstPerson.vertical: Fine-tune the tested first-person pose vertically. Zero is the recommended placement.
Fine-tune the tested first-person pose right or left. Zero is the recommended placement.
caustica.options.rt.exposureMode.tooltip: How the ray-traced image is mapped to display brightness. Auto adapts to scene luminance; Manual uses the exposure value directly.
caustica.options.rt.manualEv.tooltip: Fixed display exposure in Manual mode.
caustica.options.rt.exposureCompensation: Offsets the trusted auto-exposure target without changing the DLSS-D input.
caustica.options.rt.exposureKey.tooltip: Middle-gray luminance targeted by automatic exposure. 0.18 is the photographic standard.
caustica.options.rt.exposureLowPercentil: Darkest weighted pixels excluded from metering so empty black space cannot force the scene brighter.
caustica.options.rt.exposureHighPercenti: Upper end of the luminance population used to calculate the scene's average exposure.
caustica.options.rt.exposureHighlightPer: Very bright percentile protected independently from the average meter, such as the sun or an emitter.
caustica.options.rt.exposureHighlightHea: Maximum exposed value allowed for guarded highlights. HDR automatically limits this to the display's peak-to-paper-white ratio.
caustica.options.rt.exposureDarkAdapt.to: Seconds for exposure to complete 90% of a transition toward a brighter image after entering darkness.
Seconds for exposure to complete 90% of a transition toward a darker image after entering bright light.
caustica.options.rt.exposureMinEv.toolti: Darkest exposure scale the automatic meter may choose, expressed in photographic stops.
caustica.options.rt.exposureMaxEv.toolti: Brightest exposure scale the automatic meter may choose, expressed in photographic stops.
caustica.options.rt.exposureCenterWeight: Additional weight given to the center of the view. Zero is uniform metering; higher values resist bright edges and UI-like peripheral content.
caustica.options.rt.exposureLogMin.toolt: Lowest scene-luminance stop represented by the exposure histogram. Values below it share the first bin.
caustica.options.rt.exposureLogMax.toolt: Highest scene-luminance stop represented by the exposure histogram. Values above it share the last bin.
caustica.options.rt.sdrTonemap.tooltip: How the ray-traced image is mapped to the SDR main target. AgX preserves Caustica's existing look.
caustica.options.rt.spp.tooltip: Path-tracing samples taken per pixel each frame. Higher is cleaner but more demanding.
caustica.options.rt.maxBounces.tooltip: Maximum number of secondary path-tracing bounces after the primary hit. Higher captures more indirect light but costs more.
caustica.options.rt.astronomicalLatitude: Observer latitude for the physical Overworld sky. It changes solar altitude, seasonal day length, the celestial pole, and the paths of the Sun, Moon, and stars.
caustica.options.rt.dayOfYearOffset.tool: Earth day represented by Minecraft world day zero. Seasons then advance once per Minecraft day; day 80 is near the March equinox and day 172 is near the June solstice.
caustica.options.rt.sunSize.tooltip: Angular radius used when sampling direct sunlight. Earth's mean value is approximately 0.267 degrees; larger values cast softer penumbrae.
caustica.options.rt.moonSize.tooltip: Angular radius used when sampling direct moonlight. The Moon's mean value is approximately 0.273 degrees; larger values cast softer penumbrae.
caustica.options.rt.ambientLight.tooltip: Live environment-radiance calibration from -8 to +8 EV. This scales the sky, stars, airglow, and their transported light without adding unoccluded fill or lifting the display.
caustica.options.rt.sunlightIntensity.to: Scene-linear solar radiance calibration from -4 to +4 EV. Changes direct sunlight and solar atmospheric scattering together.
caustica.options.rt.moonlightIntensity.t: Scene-linear lunar radiance calibration from -4 to +8 EV. Changes phase-scaled moonlight and lunar atmospheric scattering without overexposing the moon texture.
caustica.options.rt.nightAirglow.tooltip: Adjusts only the bounded moonless atmospheric floor from -8 to +8 EV; it does not add unoccluded surface light.
caustica.options.rt.entities.tooltip: Include entities and block entities in the ray-traced scene.
caustica.options.rt.particles.tooltip: Draw particles as camera-ray billboards inside the traced scene.
caustica.options.rt.glow.tooltip: Draw the vanilla Glowing-effect outline (through walls) around glowing entities.
caustica.options.rt.waterWaves.tooltip: Animate water-surface normals for moving wave highlights.
caustica.options.rt.torchIntensity.toolt: Scale texture-authored torch emission up to the calibrated 2000 cd/m² peak. This changes the existing emissive transport only and updates immediately.
caustica.options.rt.psrMirrorDepth.toolt: Maximum exact mirrors followed by Primary Surface Replacement. Updates immediately; higher values cost additional rays only inside nested mirrors.
caustica.options.rt.hdr.tooltip: Present in HDR (ST.2084/PQ) instead of SDR, when the display supports it. Takes effect after restarting the game.
caustica.options.rt.hdrTonemap.tooltip: How the HDR image is mapped to the PQ swapchain. PsychoV24 is the tuned default; BT.2390 EETF remains available as the standards-based alternative.
Highlight rolloff for the PsychoV tone mapper. Higher values compress bright areas more.
caustica.options.rt.hdrPsychoShadows.too: Shadow shaping for the PsychoV tone mapper. Higher values reveal more shadow detail.
caustica.options.rt.hdrPsychoContrast.to: S-curve contrast for the PsychoV tone mapper. 1.0 is neutral.
caustica.options.rt.hdrPsychoPurity.tool: Color purity for the PsychoV tone mapper. Higher values preserve more vivid color.
caustica.options.rt.sdrPsychoPeak.toolti: Peak reference for the legacy SDR PsychoV map. Higher values preserve more highlight detail.
caustica.options.rt.sdrPsychoV23Peak.too: Scene-linear peak reference compressed into SDR white. Higher values preserve more highlight brightness and delay the shoulder, but very high values can hard-clip highlights.
caustica.options.rt.psychoV23Compression: Shared curve-to-display compression for SDR and HDR PsychoV24. Zero uses the automatic reference-range fit.
caustica.options.rt.psychoV23GamutCompre: Shared adaptive gamut-compression strength for SDR and HDR PsychoV24.
caustica.options.rt.sdrPsychoV23Compress: Curve-to-white compression power for SDR PsychoV24. Zero uses the automatic reference-range fit.
caustica.options.rt.sdrPsychoV23GamutCom: Strength of the adaptive BT.2020 gamut compression used by SDR PsychoV24.
caustica.options.rt.hdrPaperWhite.toolti: Absolute brightness (in nits) that SDR-equivalent scene brightness maps to on an HDR display.
caustica.options.rt.hdrPeak.tooltip: Absolute brightness (in nits) highlights roll off toward. Set to your display's peak HDR brightness.
caustica.options.rt.dlssQuality.tooltip: DLSS Ray Reconstruction quality mode. Lower-quality modes render fewer pixels and upscale more aggressively for higher framerates; higher-quality modes render more pixels for a sharper image. Only affects the image when DLSS Ray Reconstruction is enabled.
caustica.options.rt.dlssRr.tooltip: Use NVIDIA DLSS Ray Reconstruction to denoise and reconstruct the path-traced image. If unavailable, Caustica renders at native resolution instead.
caustica.options.rt.dlssDiffusePathGuide: A/B experiment: submit the first diffuse ray direction and world-space hit distance to DLSS Ray Reconstruction so legitimate indirect emitter samples have additional path context.
caustica.options.rt.highQualityTranspare: Use a second previous-frame refraction probe for animated water motion and retain valid refracted history. Costs extra rays only when enabled; applying the toggle rebuilds the ray pipeline automatically. The default uses a separately compiled one-ray shader with no shader overhead.
caustica.options.rt.debugView.tooltip: Visualize an intermediate ray-tracing buffer instead of the final image. Tonemapper Comparison shows the selected SDR mapper on the left and PsychoV24 on the right.
caustica.options.rt.tonemapping.tooltip: Open SDR and HDR tone-mapping controls.
caustica.options.rt.tonemapping.title: Tone Mapping
caustica.options.rt.tonemapping.resetHin: Shift+Left-click an option to restore its default.
caustica.options.rt.sdrAgxContrast.toolt: Contrast applied after AgX tone mapping. 1.0 preserves the original Caustica SDR look.
caustica.options.rt.sdrAgxSaturation.too: Saturation applied after AgX tone mapping. 1.0 preserves the original Caustica SDR look.
caustica.options.rt.sdrPbrStartCompressi: Scene-linear level where PBR Neutral starts compressing highlights toward display white.
caustica.options.rt.sdrPbrDesaturation.t: Amount of highlight desaturation used by PBR Neutral.
caustica.options.rt.sdrReinhardWhitePoin: White point for extended Reinhard. Higher values preserve more highlight headroom.
caustica.options.rt.sdrAcesExposure.tool: Input scale for the ACES fitted curve.
caustica.options.rt.sdrLottesContrast.to: Contrast exponent used by the Lottes filmic curve.
caustica.options.rt.sdrLottesShoulder.to: Highlight shoulder exponent used by the Lottes filmic curve.
caustica.options.rt.sdrLottesHdrMax.tool: Scene-linear peak reference for the Lottes filmic curve.
caustica.options.rt.sdrLottesMidIn.toolt: Input middle gray reference for the Lottes filmic curve.
caustica.options.rt.sdrLottesMidOut.tool: Output middle gray target for the Lottes filmic curve.
caustica.options.rt.sdrFrostbiteLinearEn: End of the linear range before the Frostbite shoulder begins.
caustica.options.rt.sdrFrostbiteShoulder: Strength of the Frostbite highlight shoulder.
caustica.options.rt.sdrUnchartedA.toolti: Shoulder strength coefficient for the Uncharted 2 curve.
caustica.options.rt.sdrUnchartedB.toolti: Linear strength coefficient for the Uncharted 2 curve.
caustica.options.rt.sdrUnchartedC.toolti: Linear angle coefficient for the Uncharted 2 curve.
caustica.options.rt.sdrUnchartedD.toolti: Toe strength coefficient for the Uncharted 2 curve.
caustica.options.rt.sdrUnchartedE.toolti: Toe numerator coefficient for the Uncharted 2 curve.
caustica.options.rt.sdrUnchartedF.toolti: Toe denominator coefficient for the Uncharted 2 curve.
caustica.options.rt.sdrUnchartedWhitePoi: White point used to normalize the Uncharted 2 curve.
caustica.options.rt.sdrGtContrast.toolti: Contrast exponent for the GT tone-mapping curve.
caustica.options.rt.sdrGtLinearStart.too: Start of the GT curve's middle linear segment.
caustica.options.rt.sdrGtLinearLength.to: Length of the GT curve's middle linear segment.
caustica.options.rt.sdrGtBlackCurve.tool: Toe shape exponent for the GT tone-mapping curve.
caustica.options.rt.sdrGtBlackLift.toolt: Black-level lift applied by the GT tone-mapping curve.
caustica.options.rt.hdrPsychoBleaching.t: Amount of bright-color bleaching in PsychoV.
Amount of source hue restored after PsychoV compression.
White compression curve used by PsychoV.
caustica.options.rt.hdrPsychoConeExponen: Exponent applied in the PsychoV cone-response stage.
caustica.options.rt.hdrPsychoV23Compress: Curve-to-display-peak compression power for HDR PsychoV24. Zero uses the automatic reference-range fit.
caustica.options.rt.hdrPsychoV23GamutCom: Strength of the adaptive BT.2020 gamut compression used by HDR PsychoV24.
caustica.options.rt.frameGeneration.tool: Open Streamline DLSS Frame Generation, Multi Frame Generation, and Reflex controls.
caustica.options.rt.fg.mode.tooltip: Fixed generates the selected 2x-6x multiplier. Auto is NVIDIA's legacy fixed-count fallback. Dynamic MFG requires D3D12 and is unavailable in Caustica's Vulkan renderer.
caustica.options.rt.fg.multiplier.toolti: Total displayed frames per rendered frame, limited by the current GPU and driver.
caustica.options.rt.fg.dynamicTarget.too: Target output frame rate for Dynamic mode. Auto follows the monitor refresh rate.
caustica.options.rt.fg.uiRecomposition.t: Give Streamline HUD-less color plus a full-resolution alpha-only UI mask to prevent UI ghosting in SDR and HDR.
caustica.options.rt.fg.fullscreenMenu.to: Suspend Frame Generation while a Minecraft screen is open. Chat remains generated through UI recomposition.
caustica.options.rt.fg.reflex.tooltip: Reflex Low Latency mode. DLSS Frame Generation forces effective Reflex On while active.
Development diagnostic that displays only interpolated frames.
Automatically uses slot-local Streamline timeline retirement and falls back to the blocking queue mode if the required fence contract is unavailable.
caustica.options.rt.fg.vsync.tooltip: When enabled with DLSS-G, use tear-free MAILBOX presentation with no application frame limiter. When disabled, use uncapped IMMEDIATE presentation.
caustica.options.rt.fg.diagnostics.title: DLSS Frame Generation Diagnostics
caustica.options.rt.fg.vsyncConflict: FIFO VSync is unsupported by Vulkan Streamline; MAILBOX presentation is used when available.
caustica.options.title: Caustica Settings...
caustica.options.tooltip: Open all Caustica renderer, display, capture, frame-generation, and first-person settings.
caustica.options.enabled.tooltip: Enable Caustica's native Vulkan ray-traced world renderer.
caustica.options.frameStats.tooltip: Collect detailed Caustica frame-stage timings and counters for diagnostics.
caustica.options.rt.sharc.tooltip: Disabled by default to guarantee baseline visual parity. Enabling NVIDIA SHaRC substitutes cached secondary diffuse transport; unavailable hardware falls back to the baseline tracer.
caustica.options.rt.sharcMemory.tooltip: Persistent SHaRC cache capacity. Resolve scans the full table every frame, so oversized caches cost performance as well as VRAM. Changing it rebuilds and clears the cache.
caustica.options.rt.sharcSettings.title: NVIDIA SHaRC Settings
caustica.options.rt.sharcSettings.toolti: Configure SHaRC cache sizing, voxel scale, temporal history, quantization, and firefly filtering.
caustica.options.rt.sharcSceneScale.tool: Controls voxel resolution. Smaller values make coarser voxels and more reuse but increase light leaking; changing it clears the cache.
caustica.options.rt.sharcRadianceScale.t: Integer accumulation quantization. NVIDIA recommends starting at 1000; lower it if high radiance risks overflow.
caustica.options.rt.sharcAccumulationFra: Maximum temporal accumulation window. Higher values converge more smoothly but retain lighting changes and outliers longer.
caustica.options.rt.sharcStaleFrames.too: Frames without updates before a cache entry is evicted. Very small values reduce hit rate and performance.
One rotating update path per square tile. The tuned default uses NVIDIA's 5x5 reference rate (4%); smaller tiles update faster at higher trace cost.
caustica.options.rt.sharcUpdateMaxBounce: Maximum bounces traced by sparse cache-update paths, capped by the main path depth.
caustica.options.rt.sharcMinSegmentRatio: Required path-segment length in voxel widths before a cache query is safe. NVIDIA's reference threshold is 1.0x.
caustica.options.rt.sharcGlossyQuery.too: Allow glossy cache queries only when NVIDIA's effective ray-cone formula exceeds the voxel resolution. Off is the conservative default.
caustica.options.rt.sharcLiveSecondaryDi: Evaluate view-dependent direct specular and SSS before a cache hit. On is most exact; off enables true early termination and uses cached diffuse/emissive transport at secondary vertices.
caustica.options.rt.sharcPrimaryDiffuseR: Novelty/debug C mode: show a direct nearest-voxel SHaRC lookup on eligible primary surfaces. It has no spatial interpolation or confidence filter and is expected to look blocky. Keep off for production secondary-only SHaRC; disable SHaRC for A.
caustica.options.rt.sharcDetailedStats.t: Enable expensive per-path atomic counters and diagnostic SHaRC shaders. Leave off for unbiased performance profiling.
caustica.options.rt.sharcRestoreParity.t: Restore the conservative SHaRC-on preset, disable diagnostics, and clear the cache. Live secondary direct lighting and the anti-firefly filter remain enabled.
caustica.options.rt.sharcParityNotice: Default: SHaRC On with conservative visual-parity tuning. Use debug views and the baseline toggle for validation.
caustica.options.rt.sharcAntiFirefly.too: Use NVIDIA's confidence-based high-sample-weight adjustment. This does not clamp final radiance or replace estimator validation.
